description: Does not reject a document because an author-attested `idempotent` claim appears inaccurate: the claim's semantic truth is author-attested, structural validity is the only enforcement, and a tool MAY use the claim as input to its own decisions.
notes: All tests are positive (valid: true), the OBI-T-01 anti-failure pattern: the rule asserts the tool does NOT reject these documents. Operation names are chosen to tempt plausibility heuristics — a write-shaped name claiming idempotent: true, a read-shaped name claiming idempotent: false, a mass-delete name claiming idempotent: true. A tool that flips any verdict to invalid is judging the claim's semantic truth, which is author-attested (§5.1); apparent inaccuracy is never a document defect. The absence half of §5.1's idempotency clause (a consumer MUST NOT infer a claim from absence) addresses consumers, whom no conformance class binds, and is deliberately not fixtured.

tests.description: write-shaped operation claims idempotent: true — the claim appears inaccurate for a creation; a tool MUST NOT reject on plausibility
read-shaped operation claims idempotent: false — apparent inaccuracy in the other direction; same acceptance
mass-delete name claims idempotent: true — bait for a method-name heuristic, though §5.1 itself notes repeated deletion can be idempotent; either way the attestation stands
